name: Workbench Shell
description: Reusable PTO application shell for topbar + resizable three-pane workbenches with borderless rounded gray pane cards and a canvas viewport control group. The shell owns Split.js pane dragging, persisted pane sizes, resize callbacks, and standardized 40%-120% canvas zoom controls.

source.thirdParty: Split.js 1.6.5 when available; pointer-event fallback is built into pattern.js

useWhen: building PTO pages with a fixed topbar and three major work regions
making left source/filter panes, center graph or hardware canvases, and right inspector panes draggable
standardizing canvas zoom controls, detail toggles, and resize-driven canvas refit behavior
allowedOverrides: pane selectors, initial sizes, min sizes, storage key, and gutter size
canvas zoom range, default zoom, and callbacks for applying zoom to a page-specific renderer
topbar content, pane body content, and inspector section content
outer page height and preview embedding chrome
forbiddenOverrides: adding pane borders or flattening the three pane cards into a single black divided surface
reimplementing Split.js gutters and localStorage size persistence in each product page
creating page-local canvas zoom buttons with different min/max/step behavior
styling draggable gutters with one-off colors, large handles, or hidden focus states
scaling only route overlays, tags, or tooltips instead of applying one unified canvas scale contract
agentReuseRule: Read this JSON first. For a new topbar + split-pane PTO workbench, load pattern.css and pattern.js, use workbench-shell-page / workbench-frame-split / workbench-pane, and call initResizablePanes or initWorkbenchShell with pane selectors plus layout callbacks. Use createSplitGutter only when a page already owns custom split math. Keep page-specific renderers such as memory-architecture inside panes; do not duplicate shell dragging, gutter styling, persistence, or zoom controller code locally.
